summary: ai-sdk-helpers exfiltrates developer identity on install to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and harvests the developer's identity from ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml — extracting git email, GitHub login, and GitHub email — then combines that with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), CI provider detection, and selected environment variables (GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL, EMAIL, etc.) and POSTs the payload via https.request to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app (postinstall.js line 148, payload constructed at line 124). The header comment frames this as 'anonymous diagnostics,' but the body contains direct PII with no anonymization. The package also ships scripts/publish-versions.sh, a loop that runs `npm publish` across 21 fabricated version numbers with an inline comment 'This creates the appearance of an active, maintained package' — confirming the package is a deliberate impersonation of the Vercel AI SDK ecosystem (`ai`, `@ai-sdk/openai`) seeded with fake reputation to lure installs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
